growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: pasteur.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: derived qS=mu/Yxs; mu=0.222/h, Yxs=X/S=2.9/7.2=0.403 g/g -> qS=0.222/0.403=0.551 g/gDW/h =551 mg/gDW/h /342.3 g/mol =1.61 mmol/gDW/h

sec.units: mg/mL (titer; not a steady-state specific rate in batch)

growth_records.cite: Radchenkova et al. Improved Exopolymer Production by Chromohalobacter canadensis Cultures for Its Potential Cosmeceutical Applications. Microorganisms (2020). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms8121935. [PMC7762148]
growth_records.snippet: The time course of batch fermentation continued up to the 18th hour of cultivation, when OD 660 of 3.7 (1.7 mg/mL) was measured, pO2 of 75% ... Batch | 2.9 | 7.2 | 2.34 | 806 | 0.34 | 48
growth_records.notes: Batch record (Table 1). Biomass X=2.9 mg/mL, lactose consumed S=7.2 mg/mL (initial 10 mg/mL), EP titer 2.34 mg/mL. Growth rate CALCULATED from OD: initial OD660~0.0675 (3% v/v of seed at OD660 ~2.25) to 3.7 in 18 h -> mu=ln(3.7/0.0675)/18=ln(54.8)/18=0.222 h-1; doubling=ln2/0.222=3.12 h. NOTE inconsistency: text gives 1.7 mg/mL biomass at OD660=3.7 while Table 1/2 give 2.9-3.0 mg/mL; Yxs and qS computed from Table value 2.9. Lactose qS derived via mu/Yxs (assumes exponential balanced growth). EP is excreted polymer; titer only (batch not steady-state). Temp 55C fermentation (seed grown 30C); pH rose 7.2->7.8 exp phase, ~7.4 during process.

up.method: qS=D*(S_consumed)/X=0.02*6.4/2.5=0.0512 g/gDW/h=51.2 mg/gDW/h; /342.3 g/mol =0.1496 mmol/gDW/h (S_consumed by DNS assay)

growth_records.cite: Radchenkova et al. Improved Exopolymer Production by Chromohalobacter canadensis Cultures for Its Potential Cosmeceutical Applications. Microorganisms (2020). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms8121935. [PMC7762148]
growth_records.snippet: Continuous | 0.02 | 48 | 2.5 | 6.4 | 1.62 | 647 | 0.25 | 32.34 (Table 1: D, fluid rate, X mg/mL, lactose consumed mg/mL, EP mg/mL, Y_EP/X, Y_EP/S, productivity)
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.02 h-1 (Table 1). At steady state mu=D=0.02 h-1; doubling=ln2/0.02=34.66 h. X=2.5 mg/mL, lactose consumed=6.4 mg/mL (initial 10), EP=1.62 mg/mL. Lactose qS=D*S/X=0.02*6.4/2.5=51.2 mg/gDW/h=0.150 mmol/gDW/h (lactose MW 342.3). EP specific rate=D*Y_EP/X=0.02*647=12.9 mg/gDW/h. Yxs=X/S=2.5/6.4=0.391 g/g. Y_EP/S table value 0.25 (g/g ratio; label 'mg/g' in table is a typo, since 1.62/6.4=0.253).

growth_records.cite: Radchenkova et al. Improved Exopolymer Production by Chromohalobacter canadensis Cultures for Its Potential Cosmeceutical Applications. Microorganisms (2020). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms8121935. [PMC7762148]
growth_records.snippet: 0.035 | 84 | 2.5 | 6.6 | 2.15 | 860 | 0.33 | 75.25 ... Among the tested dilution rates, the highest EP production was observed at D = 0.035 h-1 ... reaching a peak of 2.1 mg/mL
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.035 h-1 (Table 1), highest-EP condition (n=3). mu=D=0.035; doubling=19.8 h. X=2.5 mg/mL, lactose consumed=6.6 mg/mL, EP=2.15 mg/mL. Lactose qS=0.035*6.6/2.5=92.4 mg/gDW/h=0.270 mmol/gDW/h. EP specific rate=0.035*860=30.1 mg/gDW/h. Yxs=2.5/6.6=0.379 g/g.

growth_records.cite: Radchenkova et al. Improved Exopolymer Production by Chromohalobacter canadensis Cultures for Its Potential Cosmeceutical Applications. Microorganisms (2020). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms8121935. [PMC7762148]
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.05 h-1 (Table 1). mu=D=0.05; doubling=13.86 h. X=2.5 mg/mL, lactose consumed=6.1 mg/mL, EP=1.58 mg/mL. Lactose qS=0.05*6.1/2.5=122 mg/gDW/h=0.356 mmol/gDW/h. EP specific rate=0.05*633=31.65 mg/gDW/h. Yxs=2.5/6.1=0.410 g/g.

growth_records.cite: Radchenkova et al. Improved Exopolymer Production by Chromohalobacter canadensis Cultures for Its Potential Cosmeceutical Applications. Microorganisms (2020). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms8121935. [PMC7762148]
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.075 h-1 (Table 1), highest-productivity condition (n=3). mu=D=0.075; doubling=9.24 h. X=2.2 mg/mL, lactose consumed=5.3 mg/mL, EP=1.39 mg/mL. Lactose qS=0.075*5.3/2.2=180.7 mg/gDW/h=0.528 mmol/gDW/h. EP specific rate=0.075*631=47.3 mg/gDW/h. Yxs=2.2/5.3=0.415 g/g.

up.method: qS=D*S/X=0.1*3.5/0.5=0.70 g/gDW/h=700 mg/gDW/h; /342.3 =2.045 mmol/gDW/h (washout, X unreliable)

growth_records.cite: Radchenkova et al. Improved Exopolymer Production by Chromohalobacter canadensis Cultures for Its Potential Cosmeceutical Applications. Microorganisms (2020). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms8121935. [PMC7762148]
growth_records.snippet: 0.1 | 240 | 0.5 | 3.5 | 0.27 | 550 | 0.04 | 27.5 ... significant decrease at dilution rate of 0.1 h-1 (0.5 mg/mL) revealing that growth at this dilution rate was close to the washout condition
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.1 h-1 (Table 1), NEAR WASHOUT. mu=D=0.1; doubling=6.93 h. X drops to 0.5 mg/mL, lactose consumed=3.5 mg/mL, EP=0.27 mg/mL. Specific rates computed but unreliable due to washout: qS=0.1*3.5/0.5=700 mg/gDW/h=2.05 mmol/gDW/h; qP=0.1*550=55 mg/gDW/h. Yxs=0.5/3.5=0.143 g/g (depressed by washout). Flagged low confidence.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 5996 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/5996

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 5996 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/5996

phenotype_groups.label: Defined minimal medium (sole-substrate growth)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
